question: Analyze why sales dropped last quater ?
contexts:
FILE: sales_report (1).pdf
CONTENT: Quarterly sales were recorded as follows: Q1: $3.55 million Q2: $3.28 million Q3: $3.02 million Q4: $2.81 million The downward trend in Q3 and Q4 was largely associated with market competition and operational bottlenecks during the festive and year-end periods. Monthly Sales Review Monthly sales patterns showed volatility depending on category launches, promotions, and supply availability. January delivered stable post-holiday demand supported by beauty and essentials products. February remained moderate with softer electronics demand. March improved due to end-of-season campaigns. April and May saw stronger fashion-led sales, while June experienced logistics delays in North India. July and August were stable, but September through December reflected increasing pressure from competitor discount campaigns and

FILE: annual_report (1).pdf
CONTENT: growth in FY2026 and beyond. Chairman’s Statement Dear Shareholders, The financial year 2025 tested the resilience and adaptability of the retail sector. Consumer spending behavior shifted significantly toward value-conscious purchases, competitors increased promotional intensity, and fulfillment expectations became more demanding than ever before. In this environment, Apex Retail recorded consolidated revenue of $12.4 million, representing a decline of 8 percent compared with the previous year. While this outcome was below our internal expectations, it does not fully reflect the strategic progress achieved during the year. Our South India operations delivered double-digit growth, mobile commerce adoption reached record levels, and several emerging product categories

FILE: sales_report (1).pdf
CONTENT: year performance. Quarterly trends indicate a gradual slowdown across the year, with the weakest results recorded during the final quarter. Annual sales progression was influenced by the following factors: ● Competitive discounting across electronics and daily-use categories ● Lower website conversion rate due to checkout friction ● Higher cart abandonment levels ● Supply shortages in high-demand products ● Slower repeat purchase frequency ● Stronger than expected performance in fashion and beauty

FILE: sales_report (1).pdf
CONTENT: logistics delays in North India. July and August were stable, but September through December reflected increasing pressure from competitor discount campaigns and declining conversion rates. Monthly average sales ranged between $770,000 and $910,000, with December being the lowest-performing month due to delayed dispatches and high promotional competition. Category Performance Analysis Electronics Electronics remained the largest category by revenue but was the weakest performer during FY2025, declining 14 percent year-over-year. Primary causes included: ● Frequent stock-outs of fast-moving products ● Heavy competitor discounting ● Price comparison behavior among customers ● Longer delivery commitments in metro markets

FILE: fake Report.pdf
CONTENT: Apex Retail Pvt Ltd — Mini Performance Report (Q1 2026) Apex Retail Pvt Ltd recorded mixed performance in the first quarter of FY2026. Total revenue for the quarter was $3.1 million, reflecting a 5% decline compared to the previous quarter. While some regions and product categories showed improvement, overall performance remained under pressure due to operational inefficiencies and competitive market dynamics. The North region continued to underperform, with sales declining by 12%. This was primarily driven by aggressive discounting from competitors, slower delivery times in metro areas, and reduced effectiveness of digital advertising campaigns. Customer acquisition costs in this region increased significantly, further impacting profitability.

FILE: sales_report (1).pdf
CONTENT: accessories categories, lower repeat customer activity, reduced website conversion, and fulfillment delays in key markets. However, strong momentum in fashion, beauty, and South region demand partially offset these pressures. Operationally, the organization managed increased order volumes through mobile channels but encountered challenges in stock forecasting, warehouse throughput during peak seasons, and reverse logistics costs. Management has identified process improvements that are expected to enhance sales productivity and operating efficiency in FY2026. answer:
📌 Business Problem
Analyse why sales droped last quarter ?

📚 Sources
• Context retrieved from FAISS Vector DB

Problem Analysis:
Sales experienced a significant decline in the last quarter (Q4), continuing a downward trend from Q3, resulting in the weakest performance of the year. This underperformance was primarily driven by intense market competition and aggressive competitor discounting, alongside internal operational challenges such as lower website conversion rates, higher cart abandonment, and frequent stock shortages, particularly in high-demand electronics. Additionally, delivery delays and operational bottlenecks during the festive and year-end periods contributed to the reduced sales.

Key Drivers:
• Aggressive competitive discounting across electronics and daily-use categories\n• Lower website conversion rate due to checkout friction and declining conversion rates\n• Higher cart abandonment levels\n• Supply shortages and frequent stock-outs in high-demand products, especially electronics\n• Delivery delays in key markets and delayed dispatches\n• Slower repeat purchase frequency

Recommendations:
• Rebuild North region pricing competitiveness to address market competition\n• Simplify checkout process and improve payment options to enhance conversion rates and reduce cart abandonment\n• Deploy AI demand forecasting and improve supplier replenishment cycles for better inventory management and to mitigate stock shortages\n• Expand regional fulfillment hubs and improve dispatch automation to address delivery delays\n• Relaunch customer win-back campaigns to improve repeat purchase frequency
